tools.name: AdFind
tools.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors use tools like AdFind to run Active Directory queries [TA0007] and Grixba,1 an information-stealer, to enumerate network information [T1016] and scan for anti-virus software [T1518.001].

tools.name: Bloodhound
tools.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors use tools like AdFind to run Active Directory queries [TA0007] and Grixba,1 an information-stealer, to enumerate network information [T1016] and scan for anti-virus software [T1518.001].

tools.name: GMER
tools.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors use tools like GMER, IOBit, and PowerTool to disable antivirus software.

tools.name: IOBit
tools.evidence_snippet: Play actors have accessed IOBit to disable antivirus software.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors use command and control (C2) applications, including Cobalt Strike and SystemBC, and tools like PsExec to assist with lateral movement and file execution.

tools.name: PowerTool
tools.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors use tools like GMER, IOBit, and PowerTool to disable antivirus software.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.evidence_snippet: In some instances, cybersecurity researchers have observed Play ransomware actors using PowerShell scripts to target Microsoft Defender.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors use command and control (C2) applications, including Cobalt Strike and SystemBC, and tools like PsExec to assist with lateral movement and file execution.

cves.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems to gain access to networks.
Play ransomware actors exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems to gain access to networks.
Play ransomware actors exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems to gain access to networks.
Play ransomware actors exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems to gain access to networks.

Multiple ransomware groups, including initial access brokers with ties to Play ransomware operators, exploited CVE-2024-57727 in remote monitoring and management (RMM) tool SimpleHelp [T1190] to conduct remote code execution [T1059.001] at many U.S.-based entities following the vulnerabilities’ disclosure on 16 January 2025.
